questions.stem_en: When must you yield to pedestrians?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼時候你必須讓行人先行？

options.text_en: Only in marked crosswalks
options.text_zhHant: 僅在有標記的人行橫道

options.text_en: In both marked and unmarked crosswalks
options.text_zhHant: 在有標記和無標記的人行橫道上

options.text_en: Only when they wave at you
Only in school zones

questions.explanation_en: Always yield to pedestrians in marked and unmarked crosswalks. An unmarked crosswalk exists at every intersection where roads meet.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在有標記和無標記的人行橫道上必須讓行人先行。每個道路交匯的路口都存在無標記的人行橫道。

questions.stem_en: A vehicle is stopped at a crosswalk. What should you do?
questions.stem_zhHant: 一輛車停在人行橫道前。你應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Pass the stopped vehicle carefully
Honk to alert the stopped driver
options.text_zhHant: 按喇叭提醒停車的駕駛員

options.text_en: Do not pass — a pedestrian you cannot see may be crossing
options.text_zhHant: 不要超越——可能有你看不到的行人正在過馬路

options.text_en: Change lanes and speed up

questions.explanation_en: Do not pass a vehicle stopped at a crosswalk. A pedestrian you cannot see may be crossing.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 不要超越在人行橫道前停著的車輛。可能有你看不到的行人正在過馬路。

questions.stem_en: A blind pedestrian with a white cane is at a crosswalk, but the traffic signal is green for you. What should you do?
questions.stem_zhHant: 一位持白色手杖的盲人行人在人行橫道處，但交通信號燈對你是綠燈。你應該怎麼做？

options.text_en: Proceed carefully since you have the green light
options.text_zhHant: 因為你有綠燈所以小心通過

options.text_en: Honk to warn the pedestrian
Always yield to them, even if the signal is in your favor
options.text_zhHant: 必須讓他們先行，即使信號燈在你這一邊

options.text_en: Flash your headlights at them

questions.explanation_en: Blind pedestrians carry white canes or use guide dogs. You must always yield to them, even if the traffic signal is in your favor.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 盲人行人持白色手杖或使用導盲犬。即使交通信號燈在你這一邊，你也必須讓他們先行。

questions.stem_en: Why should you NOT stop in a crosswalk when waiting at a red light?
questions.stem_zhHant: 為什麼在等紅燈時不應該停在人行橫道上？

options.text_en: It is legal but discouraged
You block pedestrian traffic and put pedestrians in danger
options.text_zhHant: 你會擋住行人通行並將行人置於危險中

options.text_en: It can damage the crosswalk markings
It only matters during school hours

questions.explanation_en: Do not stop in a crosswalk when waiting at a red light or stop sign. You block pedestrian traffic and put pedestrians in danger.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在等紅燈或停車標誌時，不要停在人行橫道上。你會擋住行人通行並將行人置於危險中。

questions.stem_en: Where should you be especially watchful for pedestrians?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在哪些地方應格外注意行人？

options.text_en: Only on the freeway
Residential areas, near schools, and in parking lots
options.text_zhHant: 住宅區、學校附近和停車場

options.text_en: Only at traffic lights
Only in downtown areas

questions.explanation_en: Be especially watchful for pedestrians in residential areas, near schools, and in parking lots.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在住宅區、學校附近和停車場要格外注意行人。

questions.stem_en: A flashing 'DON'T WALK' or raised hand pedestrian signal means:
questions.stem_zhHant: 閃爍的「不要走」或舉手行人信號表示：

options.text_en: Pedestrians must stop immediately in the roadway
options.text_zhHant: 行人必須立即在路上停下

options.text_en: Do not start crossing; if already crossing, finish quickly
options.text_zhHant: 不要開始過馬路；如果已經在過，趕快走完

options.text_en: The signal is broken
Pedestrians should run across the street
options.text_zhHant: 行人應該跑步穿過街道

questions.explanation_en: A flashing DON'T WALK or raised hand signal means do not start crossing the street. If you have already started crossing, finish crossing quickly.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 閃爍的「不要走」或舉手信號表示不要開始過馬路。如果已經在過馬路中，應加快步伐走完。

questions.stem_en: When passing a bicyclist, California law requires you to give at least how much space?
questions.stem_zhHant: 超越騎自行車的人時，加州法律要求至少留出多少空間？

options.text_en: 1 foot
2 feet
3 feet
5 feet

questions.explanation_en: California law requires you to give at least 3 feet of space when passing a bicyclist. If 3 feet is not possible, slow down to a reasonable and safe speed.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 超越騎自行車的人時，加州法律要求至少留出3英尺的空間。如果無法留出3英尺，減速至合理安全的速度。

questions.stem_en: What rights do bicyclists have on the road?
questions.stem_zhHant: 騎自行車的人在道路上有什麼權利？

options.text_en: They must always ride on the sidewalk
options.text_zhHant: 他們必須始終在人行道上騎行

options.text_en: They have the same rights and responsibilities as motor vehicle drivers
options.text_zhHant: 他們與機動車駕駛員享有相同的權利和責任

options.text_en: They must yield to all motor vehicles
options.text_zhHant: 他們必須讓所有機動車先行

options.text_en: They can only ride in bike lanes
options.text_zhHant: 他們只能在自行車道上騎行

questions.explanation_en: Bicyclists have the same rights and responsibilities as motor vehicle drivers. They are entitled to share the road.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 騎自行車的人與機動車駕駛員享有相同的權利和責任。他們有權共用道路。

questions.stem_en: What should you check for before making a right turn?
questions.stem_zhHant: 右轉前你應該檢查什麼？

options.text_en: Only oncoming traffic
Bicyclists who may be riding near the right edge of the road
options.text_zhHant: 可能在道路右側邊緣騎行的自行車騎士

options.text_en: Only vehicles behind you
Only traffic signals

questions.explanation_en: Check for bicyclists before making a right turn. They may be riding in the bike lane or near the right edge of the road.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 右轉前檢查是否有騎自行車的人。他們可能在自行車道或道路右側邊緣騎行。

questions.stem_en: What is 'dooring' and why is it dangerous?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼是「開門撞人」，為什麼它很危險？

options.text_en: Leaving your car door open in a parking lot
Opening your car door into a cyclist's path, which is a common cause of bicycle accidents
options.text_zhHant: 把車門打開到騎車人的行駛路線上，這是自行車事故的常見原因

options.text_en: Slamming your car door too hard
Blocking a doorway with your car

questions.explanation_en: Dooring is opening your car door into a cyclist's path. It is a common cause of bicycle accidents. Always look carefully for bicyclists before opening your door.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 「開門撞人」是把車門打開到騎車人的行駛路線上，這是自行車事故的常見原因。開車門前要仔細觀察是否有騎自行車的人。

questions.stem_en: When is a bicyclist allowed to ride in the center of the lane?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼時候騎自行車的人可以在車道中央騎行？

options.text_en: Never — they must always stay to the right
options.text_zhHant: 永遠不行——他們必須始終靠右

options.text_en: Only on residential streets
When the lane is too narrow for a bicycle and vehicle to travel safely side by side
options.text_zhHant: 當車道太窄，自行車和機動車無法安全並排行駛時

options.text_en: Only during daylight hours

questions.explanation_en: Bicyclists are allowed to ride in the center of the lane when the lane is too narrow for a bicycle and vehicle to travel safely side by side.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 當車道太窄，自行車和機動車無法安全並排行駛時，騎自行車的人可以在車道中央騎行。

questions.stem_en: When CAN you drive in a bicycle lane?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼時候你可以在自行車道內行駛？

options.text_en: Whenever the bike lane is empty
options.text_zhHant: 只要自行車道是空的時候

options.text_en: Within 200 feet of an intersection to make a turn
options.text_zhHant: 在距路口200英尺內準備轉彎時

options.text_en: During rush hour to avoid traffic
options.text_zhHant: 在高峰期為了避開車流

options.text_en: When driving under 25 mph
options.text_zhHant: 當車速低於25英里/小時時

questions.explanation_en: You may drive in a bicycle lane only within 200 feet of an intersection to make a turn, when entering or leaving a driveway, or when parking where permitted.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 只有在距路口200英尺內準備轉彎、進出車道入口或在允許停車處停車時，才可以在自行車道內行駛。

questions.stem_en: What is a buffered bicycle lane?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼是緩衝自行車道？

options.text_en: A bike lane with a physical barrier like a wall or fence
options.text_zhHant: 有牆壁或圍欄等實體障礙的自行車道

options.text_en: A bike lane with extra space marked by chevrons or diagonal lines separating it from traffic
options.text_zhHant: 用人字形或斜線標記的額外空間將其與車流分隔的自行車道

options.text_en: A shared lane where bicycles and cars drive together
options.text_zhHant: 自行車和汽車共用的車道

options.text_en: A bike lane that is only for electric bicycles
options.text_zhHant: 僅供電動自行車使用的車道

questions.explanation_en: A buffered bicycle lane has extra painted buffer space marked with chevrons or diagonal lines to provide greater separation between bicyclists and motor vehicle traffic.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 緩衝自行車道有額外的塗漆緩衝空間，用人字形或斜線標記，為騎車人和機動車之間提供更大的分隔距離。

questions.stem_en: What is a separated bikeway?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼是分離式自行車道？

options.text_en: A bike lane with a painted buffer only
options.text_zhHant: 僅有塗漆緩衝的自行車道

options.text_en: A bike lane that is physically separated from motor vehicle traffic by a barrier, curb, or parked cars
options.text_zhHant: 透過護欄、路緣或停放的車輛與機動車道進行實體分隔的自行車道

options.text_en: A sidewalk shared with pedestrians
A lane for motorcycles and bicycles
options.text_zhHant: 供摩托車和自行車使用的車道

questions.explanation_en: A separated bikeway is a bicycle lane that is physically separated from motor vehicle traffic by a barrier, curb, parked cars, or other physical feature. Motor vehicles must not drive in separated bikeways.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 分離式自行車道是透過護欄、路緣、停放的車輛或其他實體設施與機動車道進行物理分隔的自行車道。機動車不得駛入分離式自行車道。

questions.stem_en: What do shared roadway bicycle markings (sharrows) indicate?
questions.stem_zhHant: 共用道路自行車標記（共享箭頭）表示什麼？

options.text_en: Only bicycles may use this lane
options.text_zhHant: 只有自行車可以使用這條車道

options.text_en: Bicyclists may occupy the full lane and drivers should expect to share the road
options.text_zhHant: 自行車騎士可以佔用整條車道，駕駛員應預期與其共用道路

options.text_en: This is a parking lane for bicycles
Bicyclists must ride on the sidewalk in this area
options.text_zhHant: 在此區域自行車騎士必須在人行道上騎行

questions.explanation_en: Shared roadway bicycle markings (sharrows) alert drivers that bicyclists may occupy the full lane. These markings are placed on roads where there is no separate bike lane.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 共用道路自行車標記（共享箭頭）提醒駕駛員自行車騎士可能佔用整條車道。這些標記設置在沒有獨立自行車道的道路上。

questions.stem_en: How much of a traffic lane is a motorcycle entitled to use?
questions.stem_zhHant: 摩托車有權使用多少車道空間？

options.text_en: Only the right half of the lane
The full lane
Only the bike lane
One third of the lane

questions.explanation_en: Motorcycles are entitled to the full use of a traffic lane. Do not try to share a lane with a motorcycle.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 摩托車有權使用完整的一條車道。不要試圖與摩托車共用一條車道。

questions.stem_en: Is lane splitting by motorcyclists legal in California?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在加州，摩托車車道分流合法嗎？

options.text_en: No, it is always illegal
Yes, motorcyclists may legally lane split in California
options.text_zhHant: 是的，摩托車在加州可以合法地進行車道分流

options.text_en: Only on freeways during rush hour
options.text_zhHant: 僅在高峰時段的高速公路上

options.text_en: Only when traffic is completely stopped

questions.explanation_en: Motorcyclists may legally lane split (ride between rows of stopped or slow-moving traffic) in California. Be alert for motorcycles in slow traffic.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在加州，摩托車合法地進行車道分流（在停止或緩慢行駛的車輛之間騎行）。在緩慢的車流中注意摩托車。

questions.stem_en: Why are motorcycles harder to see than cars?
questions.stem_zhHant: 為什麼摩托車比汽車更難被看到？

options.text_en: They are smaller and can be hidden in blind spots more easily
options.text_zhHant: 它們更小，更容易隱藏在盲區中

options.text_en: They always drive faster
They do not have headlights
They only drive at night

questions.explanation_en: Motorcycles are harder to see than cars due to their smaller size. Always check your mirrors and blind spots for motorcycles, especially before changing lanes or turning.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 摩托車因體積較小而比汽車更難被看到。變道或轉彎前一定檢查後視鏡和盲區是否有摩托車。

questions.stem_en: Why should you allow more following distance when behind a motorcycle?
questions.stem_zhHant: 為什麼在摩托車後方行駛時應保持更多跟車距離？

options.text_en: Motorcycles are slower than cars
Motorcycles produce exhaust fumes
Motorcycles can stop faster than cars
options.text_zhHant: 摩托車的制動距離比汽車短

options.text_en: Motorcycles swerve more often
options.text_zhHant: 摩托車更經常突然轉向

questions.explanation_en: Allow more following distance when behind a motorcycle because motorcycles can stop faster than cars.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在摩托車後方行駛時保持更多跟車距離，因為摩托車的制動距離比汽車短。

questions.stem_en: What road conditions can be major hazards for motorcyclists but minor for cars?
questions.stem_zhHant: 哪些路況對摩托車騎手是重大危險，但對汽車來說較小？

options.text_en: Speed bumps
Wide intersections
Potholes, gravel, and wet pavement
Road signs

questions.explanation_en: Road conditions that are minor for cars (potholes, gravel, wet pavement) can be major hazards for motorcyclists.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 對汽車來說較小的路況問題（坑洼、碎石、濕滑路面）對摩托車騎手來說可能是重大危險。

questions.stem_en: When may you pass a light rail vehicle on the left side?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼時候可以從左側超越輕軌車輛？

options.text_en: Whenever it is stopped to pick up passengers
Only when the tracks are so close to the right that you cannot pass on the right, on a one-way street, or when directed by a traffic officer
options.text_zhHant: 僅當軌道太靠近右側無法從右側通過、在單行道上、或交警指示時

options.text_en: Any time you can safely do so
options.text_zhHant: 任何你可以安全通過的時候

options.text_en: Only during daytime hours

questions.explanation_en: Do not pass a light rail vehicle on the left unless: tracks are too close to the right, you are on a one-way street, or a traffic officer directs you.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 不要從左側超越輕軌車輛，除非：軌道太靠近右側、在單行道上、或交警指示。

questions.stem_en: When a school bus flashes red lights and has its stop arm out on an undivided road, what must you do?
questions.stem_zhHant: 當校車在無分隔道路上閃爍紅燈並伸出停車臂時，你必須怎麼做？

options.text_en: Slow down and pass carefully
Stop from both directions and remain stopped until the red lights stop flashing
options.text_zhHant: 雙方向的車輛都必須停車，保持停車直到紅燈停止閃爍

options.text_en: Stop only if you are behind the bus
options.text_zhHant: 僅在你在校車後方時才停車

options.text_en: Honk and proceed

questions.explanation_en: When a school bus flashes red lights and has its stop arm out, you must stop from both directions on an undivided road. Remain stopped until the red lights stop flashing.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 當校車閃爍紅燈並伸出停車臂時，在無分隔道路上雙方向的車輛都必須停車。保持停車直到紅燈停止閃爍。

questions.stem_en: When do you NOT need to stop for a school bus displaying red flashing lights?
questions.stem_zhHant: 什麼情況下你不需要為閃爍紅燈的校車停車？

options.text_en: When the road is divided by a physical barrier or median
options.text_zhHant: 當道路有物理隔離帶或中央分隔帶時

options.text_en: When you are in a hurry
When it is after school hours
When there are no children visible

questions.explanation_en: If the road is divided by a physical barrier or median, you do not need to stop when approaching a school bus from the opposite direction.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果道路有物理隔離帶或中央分隔帶，從對面方向駛來時不需要為校車停車。

questions.stem_en: What is the speed limit in a school zone when children are present?
questions.stem_zhHant: 學校區域有兒童時的限速是多少？

options.text_en: 15 mph
20 mph
25 mph
30 mph

questions.explanation_en: The speed limit in a school zone is 25 mph when children are present.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 學校區域有兒童時限速25英里/小時。

questions.stem_en: What is the maximum fine for illegally passing a stopped school bus on a first offense?
questions.stem_zhHant: 初次非法越過停靠的校車的最高罰款是多少？

options.text_en: Up to $250
Up to $500
Up to $1,000
Up to $2,000

questions.explanation_en: The fine for illegally passing a stopped school bus can be up to $1,000 for a first offense.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 非法越過停靠的校車的罰款，初犯最高可達1,000美元。

questions.stem_en: What does it mean when a school bus flashes yellow lights?
questions.stem_zhHant: 當校車閃爍黃燈時是什麼意思？

options.text_en: The bus is preparing to stop — slow down and be ready to stop
options.text_zhHant: 校車正在準備停車——減速並準備停車

options.text_en: The bus is about to move — you can pass
options.text_zhHant: 校車即將移動——你可以通過

options.text_en: The bus has a mechanical problem
Children are done boarding

questions.explanation_en: When a school bus flashes yellow lights, it is preparing to stop. Slow down and be ready to stop.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 當校車閃爍黃燈時，表示正在準備停車。減速並準備停車。

questions.stem_en: Where is the largest blind spot (no-zone) on a large truck?
questions.stem_zhHant: 大卡車最大的盲區（禁區）在哪裡？

options.text_en: Directly in front
On the right side
On the left side
Directly behind

questions.explanation_en: Large trucks have bigger blind spots on all four sides. Avoid lingering in a truck's blind spots, especially on the right side.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 大卡車在四個方向都有更大的盲區。避免在卡車盲區內停留，特別是右側。

questions.stem_en: How can you tell if a truck driver can see you?
questions.stem_zhHant: 你怎麼判斷卡車司機是否能看到你？

options.text_en: If you can see the truck driver's face in their side mirror, they can see you
options.text_zhHant: 如果你在卡車的側視鏡中能看到卡車司機的臉，他們就能看到你

options.text_en: If you honk your horn
If you flash your lights
If you are within 100 feet

questions.explanation_en: If you cannot see the truck driver's face in their side mirror, the truck driver cannot see you.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 如果你在卡車的側視鏡中看不到卡車司機的臉，那麼卡車司機也看不到你。

questions.stem_en: Why should you NOT cut in front of a large truck after passing it?
questions.stem_zhHant: 為什麼超越大卡車後不要突然插到它前面？

options.text_en: It is not illegal
Trucks need much longer distances to stop than cars
options.text_zhHant: 卡車的制動距離比轎車長得多

options.text_en: Trucks can stop faster than cars
Trucks always drive very slowly

questions.explanation_en: Do not cut in front of a large truck after passing it. Trucks need much longer distances to stop than cars.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 超越大卡車後不要突然插到它前面。卡車的制動距離比轎車長得多。

questions.stem_en: A large truck signals a right turn. What should you NOT do?
questions.stem_zhHant: 大卡車打右轉燈，你不應該做什麼？

options.text_en: Slow down behind the truck
Pass on the right or squeeze between the truck and the curb
options.text_zhHant: 從右側超越或擠進卡車和路緣之間

options.text_en: Wait for the truck to complete its turn
Pass on the left if it is safe
options.text_zhHant: 如果安全的話從左側超越

questions.explanation_en: Large trucks make wide turns. Do not squeeze between a turning truck and the curb. If a truck signals a right turn, do not pass on the right.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 大卡車轉彎時幅度很大。不要擠進轉彎的卡車和路緣之間。如果卡車打右轉燈，不要從右側超越。

questions.stem_en: When following a truck uphill, why should you increase your following distance?
questions.stem_zhHant: 在上坡跟隨卡車時，為什麼應該增加跟車距離？

options.text_en: Trucks often slow significantly on steep grades
options.text_zhHant: 卡車在陡坡上通常會明顯減速

options.text_en: Trucks speed up on hills
Trucks might reverse on hills
options.text_zhHant: 卡車可能在山坡上倒車

options.text_en: Truck exhaust is more dangerous uphill
options.text_zhHant: 卡車上坡時排放更危險

questions.explanation_en: When following a truck uphill, increase your following distance. Trucks often slow significantly on steep grades.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 在上坡跟隨卡車時增加跟車距離。卡車在陡坡上通常會明顯減速。

questions.stem_en: When you see or hear an emergency vehicle with flashing lights or siren, what must you do?
questions.stem_zhHant: 當你看到或聽到閃燈或響警笛的緊急車輛時，你必須怎麼做？

options.text_en: Speed up to get out of the way
Pull to the right edge of the road and stop
Stop in the middle of the road
Continue at the same speed
options.text_zhHant: 保持相同速度繼續行駛

questions.explanation_en: When you see or hear an emergency vehicle with flashing lights or a siren, pull to the right edge of the road and stop until the emergency vehicle passes.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 看到或聽到閃燈或響警笛的緊急車輛時，靠右停車等待緊急車輛通過。

questions.stem_en: What does California's Move Over law require?
questions.stem_zhHant: 加州的「讓路法」要求什麼？

options.text_en: You must always move to the right lane on the freeway
options.text_zhHant: 你必須始終在高速公路上移到右車道

options.text_en: Change lanes or slow down when approaching any stationary vehicle on the side of the road displaying hazard lights or warning devices
options.text_zhHant: 接近路邊任何開啟危險警示燈或警示裝置的停靠車輛時，必須變道或減速

options.text_en: Move over only for police vehicles
Pull over and stop whenever you see a tow truck

questions.explanation_en: California's Move Over law requires you to change lanes or slow down when approaching ANY stationary vehicle on the side of the road — including emergency vehicles, highway maintenance vehicles, and any vehicle displaying flashing hazard lights or other warning devices. If you cannot change lanes safely, slow down to a reasonable and safe speed.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 加州的「讓路法」要求在接近路邊任何停靠車輛時，必須變道或減速——包括緊急車輛、公路養護車輛，以及任何開啟危險警示燈或其他警示裝置的車輛。如果無法安全變道，應減速至合理安全的速度。

questions.stem_en: How close can you follow a fire engine, police vehicle, or ambulance with a siren or flashing lights?
questions.stem_zhHant: 你可以跟隨開著警笛或閃燈的消防車、警車或救護車多近？

options.text_en: 100 feet
200 feet
No closer than 300 feet
500 feet

questions.explanation_en: Do not follow within 300 feet of any fire engine, police vehicle, ambulance, or other emergency vehicle with a siren or flashing lights.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 不得跟隨任何開著警笛或閃燈的消防車、警車、救護車或其他緊急車輛300英尺以內。

questions.stem_en: What symbol do slow-moving vehicles display on the back?
questions.stem_zhHant: 慢速車輛在車尾貼有什麼標誌？

options.text_en: A yellow square
An orange/red triangle
A blue circle
A green arrow

questions.explanation_en: Slow-moving vehicles (traveling 25 mph or less) display an orange/red triangle on the back.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 慢速車輛（時速25英里或以下）在車尾貼有橙色/紅色三角標誌。

questions.stem_en: Should you stop or park in a lane next to a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights?
questions.stem_zhHant: 你是否應該在亮著閃燈的停靠緊急車輛旁邊的車道上停車？

options.text_en: Yes, to offer assistance
Yes, if you slow down first
No, never stop or park next to a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights
options.text_zhHant: 不，永遠不要在亮著閃燈的停靠緊急車輛旁邊停車

options.text_en: Only if directed by an officer

questions.explanation_en: Never stop or park in a lane next to a stopped emergency vehicle with flashing lights.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 不要在亮著閃燈的停靠緊急車輛旁邊的車道上停車或停放。

questions.stem_en: Vehicles displaying diamond-shaped hazardous load signs must do what before crossing railroad tracks?
questions.stem_zhHant: 顯示菱形危險品標誌的車輛在通過鐵路道口前必須做什麼？

options.text_en: Slow down to 15 mph
Honk their horn
Come to a complete stop
Flash their headlights

questions.explanation_en: Vehicles with hazardous load placards must stop before crossing railroad tracks.
questions.explanation_zhHant: 帶有危險品標識的車輛必須在通過鐵路道口前停車。
